description: Redesign the SoundsDialog interior to a patchbay model: a split-view editor where sound files (left column) are explicitly wired to agent events (right column) via drag-and-drop. Replaces the implicit folder-name-to-event-type encoding with an explicit manifest.json per pack. Eliminates the need to copy audio files across event folders when one sound should serve multiple events.

userStories.title: manifest.json schema + read/write in soundStore
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the soundStore to read and write manifest.json from each pack directory so the patchbay has a persistent source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: PackManifest TypeScript interface: { sounds: Array<{ file: string, volume: number, enabled: boolean }>, assignments: Array<{ file: string, event: AgentEventType }> }
soundStore.loadPackManifest(packName) reads manifest.json from pack directory; returns empty manifest if file missing
soundStore.savePackManifest(packName, manifest) writes manifest.json to pack directory
soundStore.scanPacks() updated: scans pack directories for audio files AND reads manifest.json; PackInfo includes manifest data
vue-tsc -b passes

userStories.title: config.json migration — soundOverrides replaces tracks
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the config schema updated to store per-sound overrides by pack/filename key instead of category/filename.
acceptanceCriteria: SoundConfig.soundOverrides: Record<string, { volume: number, enabled: boolean }> where key is '<packName>/<filename>'
Old SoundConfig.tracks key still accepted on read (backward compat); migrated to soundOverrides on next write
effectiveVolume and isEnabled resolution follows the two-layer rule: soundOverrides → manifest default
vue-tsc -b passes

userStories.title: playForEvent updated for manifest-based lookup
userStories.description: As a user, I want sounds to play based on manifest assignments so my patchbay configuration is reflected in runtime audio.
acceptanceCriteria: playForEvent(event) looks up active pack for session → reads manifest.assignments filtered by event.type → collects enabled files → picks random → plays
Volume resolved via two-layer rule (US-002)
Missing file (in manifest but not on disk) is skipped with a console warning — no crash
Per-event cooldown still applies
vue-tsc -b passes

userStories.title: SoundsDialog — patchbay split view layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want the SoundsDialog content area to show a two-column patchbay so I can see sounds and events side by side.
acceptanceCriteria: Dialog content area (right of pack sidebar) splits into two equal columns
Left column header: 'Sounds', right column header: 'Events'
Right column shows all 16 AgentEventType values as rows
Event rows with no assignments are visually dimmed (reduced opacity or lighter text)
Event rows with assignments are full-opacity
Pack sidebar and context menu unchanged
vue-tsc -b passes

userStories.title: Left column — sound file list with inline preview
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all audio files in the selected pack and preview them inline.
acceptanceCriteria: Left column lists all files from manifest.sounds for selected pack
Each row shows: play button (left), raw filename, stop button (right)
Click play: loads and plays audio from start; if already playing, restarts from start
Click stop: stops current playback
Per-sound volume slider visible on hover
Per-sound enabled toggle (checkbox or switch) on each row
Changes to volume/enabled autosave: update manifest.sounds entry + write manifest.json
Missing file (in manifest but not on disk) shows red broken-link icon on row
vue-tsc -b passes

userStories.title: Right column — event rows with sound chips
userStories.description: As a user, I want each event row to show which sounds are assigned as removable chips.
acceptanceCriteria: Each event row shows chips for all assigned sound files
Chip label: truncated filename (max ~20 chars with ellipsis)
Chip has × button; click × removes the (file, event) assignment from manifest and autosaves
Click chip body (not ×) plays a preview of that sound inline
Live highlight: when AgentEvent fires matching an event type while dialog is open, that event row briefly flashes (200ms highlight animation)
vue-tsc -b passes

userStories.title: Drag sound from left column to event row
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag a sound file and drop it onto an event row to create an assignment.
acceptanceCriteria: Sound rows in left column are draggable (HTML5 drag API or equivalent)
During drag: ghost chip (translucent copy of filename chip) follows cursor
During drag: valid drop targets (event rows) highlight on hover
Drop onto event row: adds (file, event) to manifest.assignments if not already present; autosaves
Drop on duplicate (same file already assigned to same event): silently ignored, no feedback
Drop outside valid target: no change
vue-tsc -b passes

userStories.title: Drag chip from event row to re-assign to different sound
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag an assignment chip from an event row and drop it onto a different sound in the left column to re-assign it.
acceptanceCriteria: Assignment chips in right column are draggable
During drag: ghost chip follows cursor; left column sound rows highlight as valid targets
Drop onto sound row: removes old (originalFile, event) assignment, adds (newFile, event) assignment; autosaves
Drop onto same sound row (no-op): silently ignored
Drop outside valid target: no change, assignment remains
vue-tsc -b passes

userStories.title: Add audio files to pack — drag from Finder + file picker
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add new audio files to a pack via drag-and-drop or file picker.
acceptanceCriteria: Left column accepts file drag from OS (dragover + drop on column area)
Dropped audio files (wav/mp3/ogg/flac/m4a/webm) are copied to pack directory root
Dropped files are added to manifest.sounds with defaults: volume 1.0, enabled true
Manifest autosaved after file add
'Add file' button above left column opens Tauri file picker (dialog plugin); selected files go through same flow
Non-audio files dropped/selected are silently ignored or shown a brief error
vue-tsc -b passes

userStories.title: Pack creation — immediate create with inline rename
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create a new pack that appears immediately with an editable name.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking + in pack sidebar creates a pack directory with generated name ('New Pack', 'New Pack 2', etc.)
Empty manifest.json written to new pack directory immediately
Pack name field enters inline edit mode with cursor focused
Enter or blur confirms name; renames directory on disk
Escape cancels and deletes the newly created pack (since it's empty and user aborted naming)
vue-tsc -b passes

userStories.title: ZIP import — manifest.json required
userStories.description: As a user, I want to import sound packs from ZIP files that use the new manifest format.
acceptanceCriteria: ZIP import reads manifest.json from pack root inside ZIP
If manifest.json is absent, import fails with error message: 'This ZIP does not contain a manifest.json. Please use the new pack format.'
Audio files are extracted to pack directory root (not into subdirectories)
manifest.json is written to pack directory
Pack appears in sidebar after import
vue-tsc -b passes

userStories.title: Old pack migration — 'Migrate to new format' in context menu
userStories.description: As a user, I want to migrate old folder-structured packs to the new manifest format without losing my configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Packs without manifest.json detected on scan; labeled with a migration indicator in the sidebar
Pack context menu shows 'Migrate to new format' option for old-style packs
Migration: reads all audio files from subdirectories, creates assignments from folder names (folder name → event type), moves files to pack root, writes manifest.json, removes old subdirectories
After migration, indicator removed and pack is fully functional in patchbay
vue-tsc -b passes
